description_long: This object consists of a rectangular, textured base platform supporting two distinct clusters of solar panels. The panels are arranged in rectangular arrays, each mounted on slightly elevated frames above the base. The base itself has a patterned, ribbed surface, likely designed for structural support or drainage. The solar panels feature a grid of dark photovoltaic cells, bordered by metallic frames, and are positioned at a noticeable tilt relative to the base. The overall structure appears robust and is likely intended for installation in an open, urban environment such as a rooftop or a dedicated solar field. No visible cabling or inverter components are present, and the mounting structure appears fixed and integrated with the base.
description: A rectangular platform with two clusters of tilted solar panels mounted on a ribbed, metallic base. The panels are arranged in rectangular arrays and elevated above the surface.
description_view_0: View 0 shows the two clusters of solar panels on the ribbed base, with the larger cluster in the foreground and the smaller one in the background.
description_view_1: View 1 presents a side perspective, highlighting the tilt of the panels and the elevation of the mounting frames above the base.
description_view_2: View 2 offers a top-down angle, clearly displaying the rectangular arrangement and spacing between the two panel clusters.
description_view_3: View 3 shows the opposite side from View 1, again emphasizing the tilt and separation of the two panel groups.
attribute_panel_orientation: Panels are tilted at approximately 20-30 degrees, orientation appears fixed, azimuth direction not clearly discernible.
attribute_mounting_structure_type: ground-mounted on a ribbed metallic base with aluminum or steel frames supporting the panels.
attribute_array_configuration: Two separate rectangular clusters, each with multiple panels arranged in a grid, spaced apart on the base.
